name: UtilityAPI Energy Utility Data API
what_it_does: Developer-friendly utility data API — enabling clean energy companies, solar installers, EV charging platforms, and energy management apps to retrieve electricity usage, billing, meter data, and accounts from 50+ US utilities via customer-authorized OAuth-style utility authorization, with normalized data formats and webhook notifications.
use_cases: Retrieving historical electricity usage data via UtilityAPI for solar system sizing and proposals
Accessing real-time interval meter data via UtilityAPI for energy monitoring applications
Building EV charging optimization tools using UtilityAPI electricity rate and usage data
Automating utility bill data collection via UtilityAPI for energy management platforms
Building green energy marketplace tools via UtilityAPI for customer energy data access
not_for: Gas and water utility data (UtilityAPI focuses on electricity — use Urjanet for multi-utility)
Non-US utilities (UtilityAPI covers 50+ US utilities primarily)
Real-time smart meter streaming beyond 15-minute interval data
best_when: A solar company, EV charger platform, or energy SaaS needs customer-authorized access to their electricity usage data from US utilities — with a developer-friendly API and customer authorization flow.
avoid_when: You need gas/water data, non-US utilities, enterprise-scale without customer consent, or real-time sub-minute data.

auth.notes: API token from UtilityAPI account. Bearer token auth. Customer authorization via UtilityAPI's hosted consent flow (utility login by end user). No OAuth2 for developer access — API key. OAuth2-style for customer utility authorization. Webhook for data update notifications. Customer referral URL for authorization. Per-customer authorization tokens. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free sandbox environment; production usage-based pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Usage-based from $0.10/authorization; volume discounts; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2014 by Luke Sherron and Nick West. Y Combinator W14. Clean energy-focused. 50+ US utility integrations. Green Button standard support (DOE standard for utility data). Customer consent model — customers authorize their own utility account access. Real-time interval data (15-minute) for smart meters. Historical data going back years. Used by: solar companies (Sunrun, SunPower partners), energy platforms, load disaggregation companies. Very popular in US clean energy tech ecosystem.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with bearer token. Webhook for data updates. SOC2 compliant. Free sandbox. Green Button standard. Customer consent model — customers authorize their own utility accounts. US-only coverage (50+ utilities). Usage-based pricing. Clean energy focus.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Authorization status reads idempotent. Usage data reads idempotent by time period. Bill reads idempotent by period. Authorization creation idempotent — existing authorization returned for same customer.
known_agent_gotchas: Customer consent required — customers must log into their utility account to authorize
50+ US utilities only — check coverage for your target utilities
Data availability varies by utility (some near-real-time, others monthly batch)
Green Button data format may require parsing — not raw JSON in all cases

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 compliant. Customer consent model — data only accessible with explicit customer authorization. Green Button standard compliance. Bearer token auth. Utility data contains consumption patterns (PII). CCPA considerations for California utility data. US-only data residency. YC-backed with investor scrutiny on security practices.
